title: Fidesz–KDNP

infobox.value: * [National conservatism](/wiki/National_conservatism "National conservatism") * [Right-wing populism](/wiki/Right-wing_populism "Right-wing populism") * [Christian right](/wiki/Christian_right "Christian right")

infobox.value: [Pál Schmitt](/wiki/P%C3%A1l_Schmitt "Pál Schmitt") | 1,632,309 | 56.36 (#1) | 14 / 22 | New | [EPP](/wiki/European_People%27s_Party_Group "European People's Party Group")

([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Fidesz–KDNP pártszövetség*), formerly also known as the **Alliance of Hungarian Solidarity** ([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Magyar Szolidaritás Szövetsége*), is a right-wing [national conservative](/wiki/National_conservatism "National conservatism") [political alliance](/wiki/Political_alliance "Political alliance") of [two political parties in Hungary](/wiki/List_of_political_parties_in_Hungary "List of political parties in Hungary"), the [Fidesz – Hungarian Civic Alliance](/wiki/Fidesz "Fidesz") (Fidesz) and the [Christian Democratic People's Party](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_People%27s_Party_(Hungary) "Christian Democratic People's Party (Hungary)") (KDNP). The two parties jointly contested every national election since the [2006 parliamentary election](/wiki/2006_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2006 Hungarian parliamentary election"). The Fidesz–KDNP party alliance has governed Hungary since 2010, altogether obtaining a [supermajority](/wiki/Supermajority "Supermajority") in each of the [2010](/wiki/2010_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2010 Hungarian parliamentary election"), [2014](/wiki/2014_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2014 Hungarian parliamentary election"), [2018](/wiki/2018_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2018 Hungarian parliamentary election"), and [2022](/wiki/2022_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2022 Hungarian parliamentary election") national elections.

Hoffmann, Tamás; Gárdos-Orosz, Fruzsina (8 March 2022). ["Populism and Law in Hungary – Introduction to the Special Issue"](https://brill.com/view/journals/rela/47/1/article-p1_001.pdf) (PDF). Introduction. *Review of Central and East European Law*. **47** (1). Brill–Nijhoff: 5. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1163/15730352-bja10058](https://doi.org/10.1163%2F15730352-bja10058). [ISSN](/wiki/ISSN_(identifier) "ISSN (identifier)") [1573-0352](https://search.worldcat.org/issn/1573-0352). Retrieved 24 December 2023.

In Berberoglu, Berch (ed.). *The Global Rise of Authoritarianism in the 21st Century: Crisis of Neoliberal Globalization and the Nationalist Response* (1st ed.). New York and London: Routledge. p. 10. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.4324/9780367854379](https://doi.org/10.4324%2F9780367854379). From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Hungarian political alliance

| Founded | 10 December 2005; 20 years ago (2005-12-10) |
| [Ideology](/wiki/List_of_political_ideologies "List of political ideologies") | * [National conservatism](/wiki/National_conservatism "National conservatism") * [Right-wing populism](/wiki/Right-wing_populism "Right-wing populism") * [Christian right](/wiki/Christian_right "Christian right") |
| [Political position](/wiki/Political_spectrum "Political spectrum") | [Right-wing](/wiki/Right-wing_politics "Right-wing politics")[[1]](#cite_note-1) to [far-right](/wiki/Far-right_politics "Far-right politics")[[2]](#cite_note-2) |

| Colours | [Orange](/wiki/Orange_(colour) "Orange (colour)") |
| [National Assembly](/wiki/National_Assembly_(Hungary) "National Assembly (Hungary)") | 135 / 199 |
| [European Parliament](/wiki/European_Parliament "European Parliament") | 11 / 21 |
| [County Assemblies](/wiki/List_of_county_councils_in_Hungary "List of county councils in Hungary") | 227 / 381 |
| [General Assembly of Budapest](/wiki/General_Assembly_of_Budapest "General Assembly of Budapest") | 10 / 33 |
| * [Politics of Hungary](/wiki/Politics_of_Hungary "Politics of Hungary") * [Political parties](/wiki/List_of_political_parties_in_Hungary "List of political parties in Hungary") * [Elections](/wiki/Elections_in_Hungary "Elections in Hungary") | |

**Fidesz–KDNP Party Alliance** ([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Fidesz–KDNP pártszövetség*), formerly also known as the **Alliance of Hungarian Solidarity** ([Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language"): *Magyar Szolidaritás Szövetsége*), is a right-wing [national conservative](/wiki/National_conservatism "National conservatism") [political alliance](/wiki/Political_alliance "Political alliance") of [two political parties in Hungary](/wiki/List_of_political_parties_in_Hungary "List of political parties in Hungary"), the [Fidesz – Hungarian Civic Alliance](/wiki/Fidesz "Fidesz") (Fidesz) and the [Christian Democratic People's Party](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_People%27s_Party_(Hungary) "Christian Democratic People's Party (Hungary)") (KDNP). The two parties jointly contested every national election since the [2006 parliamentary election](/wiki/2006_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2006 Hungarian parliamentary election"). The Fidesz–KDNP party alliance has governed Hungary since 2010, altogether obtaining a [supermajority](/wiki/Supermajority "Supermajority") in each of the [2010](/wiki/2010_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2010 Hungarian parliamentary election"), [2014](/wiki/2014_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2014 Hungarian parliamentary election"), [2018](/wiki/2018_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2018 Hungarian parliamentary election"), and [2022](/wiki/2022_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "2022 Hungarian parliamentary election") national elections.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fidesz%E2%80%93KDNP&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The two parties formed their permanent electoral coalition on 10 December 2005.[[3]](#cite_note-3) After the 2006 election, Fidesz and KDNP separately formed parliamentary groups, but they established a caucus alliance in the Hungarian parliament.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Technically Fidesz and KDNP are a coalition, but many consider KDNP to actually be a satellite party of Fidesz,[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) since it has been unable to get into the Parliament on its own since [1994](/wiki/1994_Hungarian_parliamentary_election "1994 Hungarian parliamentary election") when it barely passed the [election threshold](/wiki/Election_threshold "Election threshold") of 5% of votes. Without Fidesz, its support cannot be measured,[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) and even a leading Fidesz politician, [János Lázár](/wiki/J%C3%A1nos_L%C3%A1z%C3%A1r "János Lázár") stated in 2011 that Fidesz does not consider the government to be a coalition government.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On 3 March 2021, the Fidesz left the [European People's Party Parliamentary Group](/wiki/European_People%27s_Party_group "European People's Party group"), while KDNP remained a member.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In response to the admission of the [Tisza Party](/wiki/Respect_and_Freedom_Party "Respect and Freedom Party") to the EPP following the [2024 European Parliament election](/wiki/2024_European_Parliament_election "2024 European Parliament election"), the KDNP decided to leave the EPP and its parliamentary group on 18 June 2024.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Fidesz and KDNP would form the [Patriots for Europe](/wiki/Patriots_for_Europe "Patriots for Europe") group in the European Parliament.
